-> Result<Json<Value>, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n\n    for group in topology.groups() {\n        if let Some(node_id) = group.nodes().first() {\n            let request = ScatterRequest {\n                method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n                path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n                body: vec![],\n                headers: vec![],\n            };\n\n            let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n\n            let result = scatter\n                .scatter(&topology, vec![node_id.clone()], request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n                .await\n                .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n\n            if let Some(resp) = result.responses.first() {\n                if resp.status == 200 {\n                    let value: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&resp.body)\n                        .map_err(|_| ErrorResponse::internal_error(\"Invalid JSON response\"))?;\n                    return Ok(Json(value));\n                } else if resp.status == 404 {\n                    return Err(ErrorResponse::index_not_found(index));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Err(ErrorResponse::index_not_found(index))\n}\n\n/// Generic DELETE handler for resetting a setting to default.\nasync fn delete_setting(\n    state: &ProxyState,\n    index: &str,\n    setting_path: &str,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let topology = state.topology().await;\n    let targets = write_targets(0, &topology);\n\n    if targets.is_empty() {\n        return Err(ErrorResponse::internal_error(\"No nodes available\"));\n    }\n\n    let request = ScatterRequest {\n        method: \"DELETE\".to_string(),\n        path: format!(\"/indexes/{}/{}\", index, setting_path),\n        body: vec![],\n        headers: vec![],\n    };\n\n    let scatter = HttpScatter::new((*state.client).clone(), state.config.server.request_timeout_ms);\n    let result = scatter\n        .scatter(&topology, targets, request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n        .await\n        .map_err(|e| ErrorResponse::internal_error(e.to_string()))?;\n\n    if let Some(resp) = result.responses.first() {\n        let status = axum::http::StatusCode::from_u16(resp.status).unwrap_or(axum::http::StatusCode::OK);\n        let value: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&resp.body)\n            .unwrap_or_else(|_| Value::Null);\n        return Ok((status, Json(value)).into_response());\n    }\n\n    Ok((axum::http::StatusCode::ACCEPTED, Json(serde_json::json!({}))).into_response())\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_filterable_attributes_injection() {\n        let input = serde_json::json!([\"title\", \"description\"]);\n        let mut updated = input.clone();\n\n        if let Some(arr) = updated.as_array_mut() {\n            if !arr.iter().any(|v| v.as_str() == Some(\"_miroir_shard\")) {\n                arr.push(serde_json::json!(\"_miroir_shard\"));\n            }\n        }\n\n        let expected = serde_json::json!([\"title\", \"description\", \"_miroir_shard\"]);\n        assert_eq!(updated, expected);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_filterable_attributes_already_present() {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_10e60b4b509c444f929b6da3","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Bearer-token dispatch per plan §5\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Implements rules 2-5 for master-key/admin-key bearer dispatch:\n4\t//! - Rule 2: master-key can access all endpoints (full admin access)\n5\t//! - Rule 3: admin-key can access admin-only endpoints (/admin/*, /_miroir/*)\n6\t//! - Rule 4: No bearer token → public endpoints only (/health, /version)\n7\t//! - Rule 5: Invalid token → 403 Forbidden\n8\t\n9\tuse axum::{\n10\t    extract::State,\n11\t    http::HeaderMap,\n12\t    middleware::Next,\n13\t    response::Response,\n14\t};\n15\tuse crate::state::ProxyState;\n16\tuse crate::error_response::ErrorResponse;\n17\t\n18\t/// Token kind determined from the bearer token.\n19\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n20\tpub enum TokenKind {\n21\t    /// Master key - full access to all endpoints.\n22\t    Master,\n23\t    /// Admin key - access to admin endpoints only.\n24\t    Admin,\n25\t}\n26\t\n27\t/// Authentication result from bearer token validation.\n28\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n29\tpub enum AuthResult {\n30\t    /// Valid token with its kind.\n31\t    Valid(TokenKind),\n32\t    /// No bearer token present.\n33\t    None,\n34\t    /// Invalid bearer token.\n35\t    Invalid,\n36\t}\n37\t\n38\t/// Validate bearer token against the configured keys.\n39\tpub fn validate_bearer_token(headers: &HeaderMap, state: &ProxyState) -> AuthResult {\n40\t    let auth_header = match headers.get(\"authorization\") {\n41\t        Some(h) => h,\n42\t        None => return AuthResult::None,\n43\t    };\n44\t\n45\t    let auth_str = match auth_header.to_str() {\n46\t        Ok(s) => s,\n47\t        Err(_) => return AuthResult::Invalid,\n48\t    };\n49\t\n50\t    let token = match auth_str.strip_prefix(\"Bearer \") {\n51\t        Some(t) => t,\n52\t        None => return AuthResult::None,\n53\t    };\n54\t\n55\t    // Check master key first (rule 2)\n56\t    if state.is_valid_master_key(token) {\n57\t        return AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Master);\n58\t    }\n59\t\n60\t    // Check admin key (rule 3)\n61\t    if state.is_valid_admin_key(token) {\n62\t        return AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Admin);\n63\t    }\n64\t\n65\t    // Invalid token (rule 5)\n66\t    AuthResult::Invalid\n67\t}\n68\t\n69\t/// Check if a path requires authentication.\n70\tpub fn requires_auth(path: &str) -> bool {\n71\t    // Public endpoints (rule 4)\n72\t    if path == \"/health\" || path == \"/version\" {\n73\t        return false;\n74\t    }\n75\t\n76\t    true\n77\t}\n78\t\n79\t/// Check if a path requires admin access.\n80\tpub fn requires_admin(path: &str) -> bool {\n81\t    // Admin endpoints (rule 3)\n82\t    if path.starts_with(\"/admin/\") || path.starts_with(\"/_miroir/\") {\n83\t        return true;\n84\t    }\n85\t\n86\t    // /metrics endpoint requires admin\n87\t    if path == \"/metrics\" {\n88\t        return true;\n89\t    }\n90\t\n91\t    false\n92\t}\n93\t\n94\t/// Authentication middleware.\n95\t///\n96\t/// Enforces bearer token validation per plan §5 rules 2-5.\n97\tpub async fn auth_middleware(\n98\t    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n99\t    req: axum::extract::Request,\n100\t    next: Next,\n101\t) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n102\t    let path = req.uri().path();\n103\t\n104\t    // Check if authentication is required\n105\t    if !requires_auth(path) {\n106\t        return Ok(next.run(req).await);\n107\t    }\n108\t\n109\t    // Validate bearer token\n110\t    let auth_result = validate_bearer_token(req.headers(), &state);\n111\t\n112\t    match auth_result {\n113\t        AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Master) => {\n114\t            // Master key has full access (rule 2)\n115\t            Ok(next.run(req).await)\n116\t        }\n117\t        AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Admin) => {\n118\t            // Admin key can only access admin endpoints (rule 3)\n119\t            if requires_admin(path) {\n120\t                Ok(next.run(req).await)\n121\t            } else {\n122\t                Err(ErrorResponse::new(\n123\t                    \"Admin key cannot access this endpoint. Use master key.\",\n124\t                    \"invalid_api_key\",\n125\t                ))\n126\t            }\n127\t        }\n128\t        AuthResult::None => {\n129\t            // No bearer token → 401 (rule 4)\n130\t            Err(ErrorResponse::new(\n131\t                \"Missing Authorization header\",\n132\t                \"missing_authorization_header\",\n133\t            ))\n134\t        }\n135\t        AuthResult::Invalid => {\n136\t            // Invalid token → 403 (rule 5)\n137\t            Err(ErrorResponse::new(\n138\t                \"Invalid API key\",\n139\t Bearer-token dispatch per plan §5\n//!\n//! Implements rules 2-5 for master-key/admin-key bearer dispatch:\n//! - Rule 2: master-key can access all endpoints (full admin access)\n//! - Rule 3: admin-key can access admin-only endpoints (/admin/*, /_miroir/*)\n//! - Rule 4: No bearer token → public endpoints only (/health, /version)\n//! - Rule 5: Invalid token → 403 Forbidden\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::State,\n    http::HeaderMap,\n    middleware::Next,\n    response::Response,\n};\nuse crate::state::ProxyState;\nuse crate::error_response::ErrorResponse;\n\n/// Token kind determined from the bearer token.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum TokenKind {\n    /// Master key - full access to all endpoints.\n    Master,\n    /// Admin key - access to admin endpoints only.\n    Admin,\n}\n\n/// Authentication result from bearer token validation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum AuthResult {\n    /// Valid token with its kind.\n    Valid(TokenKind),\n    /// No bearer token present.\n    None,\n    /// Invalid bearer token.\n    Invalid,\n}\n\n/// Validate bearer token against the configured keys.\npub fn validate_bearer_token(headers: &HeaderMap, state: &ProxyState) -> AuthResult {\n    let auth_header = match headers.get(\"authorization\") {\n        Some(h) => h,\n        None => return AuthResult::None,\n    };\n\n    let auth_str = match auth_header.to_str() {\n        Ok(s) => s,\n        Err(_) => return AuthResult::Invalid,\n    };\n\n    let token = match auth_str.strip_prefix(\"Bearer \") {\n        Some(t) => t,\n        None => return AuthResult::None,\n    };\n\n    // Check master key first (rule 2)\n    if state.is_valid_master_key(token) {\n        return AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Master);\n    }\n\n    // Check admin key (rule 3)\n    if state.is_valid_admin_key(token) {\n        return AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Admin);\n    }\n\n    // Invalid token (rule 5)\n    AuthResult::Invalid\n}\n\n/// Check if a path requires authentication.\npub fn requires_auth(path: &str) -> bool {\n    // Public endpoints (rule 4)\n    if path == \"/health\" || path == \"/version\" {\n        return false;\n    }\n\n    true\n}\n\n/// Check if a path requires admin access.\npub fn requires_admin(path: &str) -> bool {\n    // Admin endpoints (rule 3)\n    if path.starts_with(\"/admin/\") || path.starts_with(\"/_miroir/\") {\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    // /metrics endpoint requires admin\n    if path == \"/metrics\" {\n        return true;\n    }\n\n    false\n}\n\n/// Authentication middleware.\n///\n/// Enforces bearer token validation per plan §5 rules 2-5.\npub async fn auth_middleware(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    req: axum::extract::Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Result<Response, ErrorResponse> {\n    let path = req.uri().path();\n\n    // Check if authentication is required\n    if !requires_auth(path) {\n        return Ok(next.run(req).await);\n    }\n\n    // Validate bearer token\n    let auth_result = validate_bearer_token(req.headers(), &state);\n\n    match auth_result {\n        AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Master) => {\n            // Master key has full access (rule 2)\n            Ok(next.run(req).await)\n        }\n        AuthResult::Valid(TokenKind::Admin) => {\n            // Admin key can only access admin endpoints (rule 3)\n            if requires_admin(path) {\n                Ok(next.run(req).await)\n            } else {\n                Err(ErrorResponse::new(\n                    \"Admin key cannot access this endpoint. Use master key.\",\n                    \"invalid_api_key\",\n                ))\n            }\n        }\n        AuthResult::None => {\n            // No bearer token → 401 (rule 4)\n            Err(ErrorResponse::new(\n                \"Missing Authorization header\",\n                \"missing_authorization_header\",\n            ))\n        }\n        AuthResult::Invalid => {\n            // Invalid token → 403 (rule 5)\n            Err(ErrorResponse::new(\n                \"Invalid API key\",\n                \"invalid_api_key\",\n            ))\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\n    use miroir_core::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n\n    fn test_state() -> ProxyState {\n        let mut std::sync::Arc::new(tokio::sync::RwLock::new(topology)),\n            client: std::sync::Arc::new(crate::client::NodeClient::new(\n                \"node-key\".to_string(),\n                &Default::default(),\n            )),\n            query_seq: std::sync::Arc::new(std::sync::atomic::AtomicU64::new(0)),\n            master_key: std::sync::Arc::new(\"test-master-key\".to_string()),\n            admin_key: std::sync::Arc::new(\"test-admin-key\".to_string()),\n            metrics: std::sync::Arc::new(crate::middleware::Metrics::new()),\n            task_manager: std::sync::Arc::new(crate::task_manager::TaskManager::new()),\n            retry_cache: std::sync::Arc::new(crate::retry_cache::RetryCache::new(\n                std::time::Duration::from_secs(60),\n            )),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn make_headers(token: Option<&str>) -> HeaderMap {\n        let mut headers = HeaderMap::new();\n        if let Some(t) = token {\n            headers.insert(\"authorization\", format!(\"Bearer {t}\").parse().unwrap());\n        }\n        headers\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_validate_master_key() {\n        let state = test_state();\n        let headers = make_headers(Some(\"test-master-key\"));\n\n        let result = Tracing/logging + Prometheus middleware\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::{Request, State},\n    middleware::Next,\n    response::Response,\n};\nuse crate::state::ProxyState;\nuse std::time::Instant;\nuse prometheus::{Counter, Histogram, IntGauge, Registry, TextEncoder, HistogramOpts, Encoder};\n\n/// Prometheus metrics registry.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct Metrics {\n    pub registry: Registry,\n    requests_total: Counter,\n    request_duration_seconds: Histogram,\n    requests_in_flight: IntGauge,\n    degraded_requests_total: Counter,\n    no_quorum_requests_total: Counter,\n}\n\nimpl Metrics {\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        let registry = Registry::new();\n\n        // Create and register metrics\n        let requests_total = Counter::new(\n            \"miroir_requests_total\",\n            \"Total number of requests\"\n        ).unwrap();\n\n        let request_duration_seconds = Histogram::with_opts(HistogramOpts {\n            common_opts: prometheus::Opts::new(\n                \"miroir_request_duration_seconds\",\n                \"Request duration in seconds\"\n            ),\n            buckets: vec![0.005, 0.01, 0.025, 0.05, 0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1.0, 2.5, 5.0, 10.0],\n        }).unwrap();\n\n        let requests_in_flight = IntGauge::new(\n            \"miroir_requests_in_flight\",\n            \"Current number of requests in flight\"\n        ).unwrap();\n\n        let degraded_requests_total = Counter::new(\n            \"miroir_degraded_requests_total\",\n            \"Total number of degraded requests\"\n        ).unwrap();\n\n        let no_quorum_requests_total = Counter::new(\n            \"miroir_no_quorum_requests_total\",\n            \"Total number of requests that failed with no quorum\"\n        ).unwrap();\n\n        // Register all metrics\n        registry.register(Box::new(requests_total.clone())).unwrap();\n        registry.register(Box::new(request_duration_seconds.clone())).unwrap();\n        registry.register(Box::new(requests_in_flight.clone())).unwrap();\n        registry.register(Box::new(degraded_requests_total.clone())).unwrap();\n        registry.register(Box::new(no_quorum_requests_total.clone())).unwrap();\n\n        Self {\n            registry,\n            requests_total,\n            request_duration_seconds,\n            requests_in_flight,\n            degraded_requests_total,\n            no_quorum_requests_total,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Record a request with labels.\n    pub fn record_request(&self, _method: &str, _path: &str, status: u16, duration_secs: f64) {\n        self.requests_total.inc();\n        self.request_duration_seconds.observe(duration_secs);\n\n        // Check for no quorum (503 status)\n        if status == 503 {\n            self.no_quorum_requests_total.inc();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Record a degraded request.\n    pub fn record_degraded(&self, _method: &str, _path: &str) {\n        self.degraded_requests_total.inc();\n    }\n\n    /// Increment requests in flight.\n    pub fn inc_in_flight(&self) {\n        self.requests_in_flight.inc();\n    }\n\n    /// Decrement requests in flight.\n    pub fn dec_in_flight(&self) {\n        self.requests_in_flight.dec();\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for Metrics {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Tracing middleware that logs each request.\npub async fn tracing_middleware(req: Request, next: Next) -> Response {\n    let method = req.method().clone();\n    let uri = req.uri().clone();\n    let start = Instant::now();\n\n    let response = next.run(req).await;\n\n    let duration = start.elapsed();\n    let status = response.status();\n\n    tracing::info!(\n        method = %method,\n        uri = %uri,\n        status = status.as_u16(),\n        duration_ms = duration.as_millis(),\n        \"request completed\"\n    );\n\n    response\n}\n\n/// Prometheus metrics middleware.\npub async fn prometheus_middleware(\n    State(state): State<ProxyState>,\n    req: Request,\n    next: Next,\n) -> Response {\n    let method = req.method().to_string();\n    let path = req.uri().path().to_string();\n\n    state.metrics.inc_in_flight();\n    let start = Instant::now();\n\n    let response = next.run(req).await;\n\n    let duration = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();\n    let status = response.status().as_u16();\n\n    // Record metrics\n    state.metrics.record_request(&method, &path, status, duration);\n    state.metrics.dec_in_flight();\n\n    // Check for degraded header\n    if response.headers().get(\"X-Miroir-Degraded\").is_some() {\n        state.metrics.record_degraded(&method, &path);\n    }\n\n Error-format parity test\n//! - GET /_miroir/topology matches expected shape\n\nuse std::collections::HashSet;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n#[derive(Clone)]\nstruct TestNode {\n    id: String,\n    base_url: String,\n}\n\nimpl TestNode {\n    fn new(id: impl Into<String>, port: u16) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            id: id.into(),\n            base_url: format!(\"http://127.0.0.1:{}\", port),\n        }\n    }\n\n    async fn get(&self, path: &str) -> reqwest::Response {\n        let client = reqwest::Client::new();\n        client\n            .get(format!(\"{}{}\", self.base_url, path))\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .unwrap()\n    }\n\n    async fn post(&self, path: &str, body: serde_json::Value) -> reqwest::Response {\n        let client = reqwest::Client::new();\n        client\n            .post(format!(\"{}{}\", self.base_url, path))\n            .json(&body)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .unwrap()\n    }\n\n    async fn delete(&self, path: &str) -> reqwest::Response {\n        let client = reqwest::Client::new();\n        client\n            .delete(format!(\"{}{}\", self.base_url, path))\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .unwrap()\n    }\n}\n\nstruct TestCluster {\n    proxy_url: String,\n    nodes: Vec<TestNode>,\n}\n\nimpl TestCluster {\n    fn new(proxy_port: u16, node_ports: Vec<u16>) -> Self {\n        let nodes = node_ports\n            .into_iter()\n            .enumerate()\n            .map(|(i, port)| TestNode::new(format!(\"node-{}\", i), port))\n            .collect();\n\n        Self {\n            proxy_url: format!(\"http://127.0.0.1:{}\", proxy_port),\n            nodes,\n        }\n    }\n\n    async fn create_index(&self, uid: &str, primary_key: Option<&str>) -> reqwest::Response {\n        let client = reqwest::Client::new();\n        let mut body = serde_json::json!({ \"uid\": uid });\n        if let Some(pk) = primary_key {\n            body[\"primaryKey\"] = serde_json::json!(pk);\n        }\n        client\n            .post(format!(\"{}/indexes\", self.proxy_url))\n            .json(&body)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .unwrap()\n    }\n\n    async fn add_documents(&self, index: &str, documents: Vec<serde_json::Value>) -> reqwest::Response {\n        let client = reqwest::Client::new();\n        client\n            .post(format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/documents\", self.proxy_url, index))\n            .json(&documents)\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .unwrap()\n    }\n\n    async fn search(&self, index: &str, query: serde_json::Value) -> reqwest::Response {\n        let client = .unwrap()\n    }\n\n    async fn get_document(&self, index: &str, id: &str) -> reqwest::Response {\n        let client = reqwest::Client::new();\n        client\n            .get(format!(\n                \"{}/indexes/{}/documents/{}\",\n                self.proxy_url, index, id\n            ))\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .unwrap()\n    }\n\n    async fn .get(format!(\"{}/indexes/{}/stats\", self.proxy_url, index))\n            .send()\n            .await\n            .unwrap()\n    }\n}\n\n/// Test: 1000 documents indexed across 3 nodes, each retrievable by ID\n#[tokio::test]\n#[ignore] // Requires running nodes\nasync fn test_1000_documents_indexed_retrievable_by_id() {\n    let cluster = TestCluster::new(7700, vec![7701, 7702, 7703]);\n\n    // Create index\n    let create_resp = cluster.create_index(\"test_index\", Some(\"id\")).await;\n    assert!(create_resp.status().is_success());\n\n    // Wait for index creation\n serde_json::json!({\n                \"id\": format!(\"doc-{:05}\", i),\n                \"title\": format!(\"Document {}\", i),\n                \"value\": i,\n            })\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    // Add documents in batches\n    for chunk in documents.chunks(100) {\n        let resp = cluster.add_documents(\"test_index\", chunk.to_vec()).await;\n        assert!(resp.status().is_success());\n    }\n\n    // Wait for indexing\n    tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_secs(2)).await;\n\n    // Verify each document is retrievable by ID\n    for assert_eq!(doc[\"id\"], id);\n        assert_eq!(doc[\"value\"], i);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Test: Unique-keyword search finds every doc exactly once\n#[tokio::test]\n#[ignore]\nasync fn test_unique_keyword_search_finds_all_docs_once() {\n    let cluster = TestCluster::new(7700, vec![7701, 7702, 7703]);\n\n    // Create index\n    let create_resp = cluster.create_index(\"search_test\", Some(\"id\")).await;\n    assert!(create_resp.status().is_success());\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(500)).await;\n\n    // Create documents with unique keywords\n    let documents: Vec<serde_json::Value> = (0..100)\n        .map(|i| {\n            serde_json::json!({\n                \"id\": format!(\"unique-doc-{}\", i),\n                \"keyword\": format!(\"unique-keyword-{}\", i),\n                \"value\": i,\n            })\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    let resp = cluster.add_documents(\"search_test\", documents).await;\n    assert!(resp.status().is_success());\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_secs(1)).await;\n\n    // Search for each unique keyword and verify exactly one result\n    for i in 0..100 {\n        let keyword = format!(\"unique-keyword-{}\", i);\n        let search_resp = cluster\n            .search(\n                \"search_test\",\n                serde_json::json!({ \"q\": keyword, \"limit\": 100 }),\n            )\n            .await;\n\n        assert!(search_resp.status().is_success());\n\n        let results: serde_json::Value = search_resp.json().await.unwrap();\n        let hits = results[\"hits\"].as_array().unwrap();\n\n        assert_eq!(\n            hits.len(),\n            1,\n            \"Expected exactly 1 result for keyword {}, got {}\",\n            keyword,\n            hits.len()\n        );\n\n        assert_eq!(hits[0][\"keyword\"], keyword);\n        assert_eq!(hits[0][\"value\"], i);\n    }\n\n    // Search without query should return all docs\n    let all_resp = cluster\n        .search(\"search_test\", serde_json::json!({ \"q\": \"\", \"limit\": 200 }))\n        .await;\n\n    let all_results: serde_json::Value = all_resp.json().await.unwrap();\n    let all_hits = all_results[\"hits\"].as_array().unwrap();\n\n    // Check that we have 100 unique documents\n    let mut seen_ids = HashSet::new();\n    for hit in all_hits {\n        let id = hit[\"id\"].as_str().unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            seen_ids.insert(id),\n            \"Duplicate document ID found: {}\",\n            id\n        );\n    }\n\n    assert_eq!(seen_ids.len(), 100, \"Expected 100 unique documents\");\n}\n\n/// Test: Facet aggregation across 3 color values sums correctly\n#[tokio::test]\n#[ignore]\nasync fn test_facet_aggregation_sums_correctly() {\n    let cluster = TestCluster::new(7700, vec![7701, 7702, 7703]);\n\n    // Create index with filterable attributes\n    let create_resp = cluster.create_index(\"facet_test\", Some(\"id\")).await;\n    assert!(create_resp.status().is_success());\n\n .post(format!(\"{}/indexes/facet_test/settings/filterable-attributes\", cluster.proxy_url))\n        .json(&serde_json::json!([\"id\", \"color\", \"_miroir_shard\"]))\n        .send()\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n    assert!(filter_resp.status().is_success());\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(500)).await;\n\n    // Create documents with 3 color values distributed across shards\n    let documents: Vec<serde_json::Value> = (0..300)\n        .map(|i| {\n            let color = match i % 3 {\n                0 => \"red\",\n                1 => \"blue\",\n                _ => \"green\",\n            };\n            serde_json::json!({\n                \"id\": format!(\"color-doc-{}\", i),\n                \"color\": color,\n                \"value\": i,\n            })\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    let resp = cluster.add_documents(\"facet_test\", documents).await;\n    assert!(resp.status().is_success());\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_secs(2)).await;\n\n    // Search with facets on color\n    let search_resp = cluster\n        .search(\n            \"facet_test\",\n            serde_json::json!({\n                \"q\": \"\",\n                \"facets\": [\"color\"],\n                \"limit\": 0\n            }),\n        )\n        .await;\n\n    assert!(search_resp.status().is_success());\n\n    let results: serde_json::Value = search_resp.json().await.unwrap();\n    let facet_dist = results[\"facetDistribution\"][\"color\"].as_object().unwrap();\n\n    // Verify each color has exactly 100 documents\n    assert_eq!(\n        facet_dist.get(\"red\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()),\n        Some(100),\n        \"Expected 100 red documents\"\n    );\n    assert_eq!(\n        facet_dist.get(\"blue\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()),\n        Some(100),\n        \"Expected 100 blue documents\"\n    );\n    assert_eq!(\n        facet_dist.get(\"green\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()),\n        Some(100),\n        \"Expected 100 green documents\"\n    );\n}\n\n/// Test: Offset/limit paging preserves global ordering\n#[tokio::test]\n#[ignore]\nasync fn test_offset_limit_paging_preserves_global_ordering() {\n    let cluster = TestCluster::new(7700, vec![7701, 7702, 7703]);\n\n    // Create index\n    let create_resp = cluster.create_index(\"paging_test\", Some(\"id\")).await;\n    assert!(create_resp.status().is_success());\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_millis(500)).await;\n\n    // Create documents with sequential values\n    let documents: Vec<serde_json::Value> = (0..100)\n        .map(|i| {\n            serde_json::json!({\n                \"id\": format!(\"paging-doc-{:03}\", i),\n                \"value\": i,\n                \"text\": \"same text for all\",\n            })\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    let resp = cluster.add_documents(\"paging_test\", documents).await;\n    assert!(resp.status().is_success());\n\n    tokio::time::sleep(tokio::time::Duration::from_secs(1)).await;\n\n    // Fetch all documents in pages\n    let mut all_values: Vec<i64> = Vec::new();\n    let page_size = 10;\n\n    for page in 0..10 {\n        let offset = page * page_size;\n        let search_resp = cluster\n            .search(\n                \"paging_test\",\n                serde_json::json!({\n                    \"q\": \"same text\",\n                    \"limit\": page_size,\n                    \"offset\": offset\n                }),\n            )\n            .await;\n\n assert_eq!(\n            hits.len(),\n            page_size,\n            \"Expected {} results on page {}\",\n            page_size,\n            page\n        );\n\n        for hit in hits {\n            let value = hit[\"value\"].as_i64().unwrap();\n            all_values.push(value);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Verify we got exactly 100 unique values\n    assert_eq!(all_values.len(), 100);\n\n    // Verify global ordering is preserved (no duplicates, all 0-99 present)\n    let mut seen = HashSet::new();\n    for value in all_values {\n        assert!(\n            seen.insert(value),\n            \"Duplicate value found in paging: {}\",\n            value\n        );\n    }\n\n    for i in 0..100 {\n        assert!(seen.contains(&i), \"Missing value {} in results\", i);\n    }\n}\n\n/// HTTP scatter-gather execution for Meilisearch nodes.\n\nuse crate::client::NodeClient;\nuse crate::retry_cache::{CachedResponse, RetryCache};\nuse async_trait::async_trait;\nuse miroir_core::config::UnavailableShardPolicy;\nuse miroir_core::scatter::{NodeResponse, Scatter, ScatterRequest, ScatterResponse};\nuse miroir_core::topology::{NodeId, Topology};\nuse miroir_core::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse std::time::Duration;\nuse tokio::time::timeout;\n\n/// HTTP scatter implementation using NodeClient.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct HttpScatter {\n    client: NodeClient,\n    node_timeout_ms: u64,\n    /// Retry cache for idempotency (plan §4).\n    retry_cache: Option<RetryCache>,\n}\n\nimpl HttpScatter {\n    pub fn new(client: NodeClient, node_timeout_ms: u64) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            client,\n            node_timeout_ms,\n            retry_cache: None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new HttpScatter with retry cache enabled.\n    pub fn with_retry_cache(client: NodeClient, node_timeout_ms: u64, retry_cache: RetryCache) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            client,\n            node_timeout_ms,\n            retry_cache: Some(retry_cache),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Send a request to a single node with retry cache lookup.\n    async fn send_to_node_with_cache(\n        &self,\n        topology: &Topology,\n        node_id: &NodeId,\n        method: &str,\n        path: &str,\n        body: &[u8],\n        headers: &[(String, String)],\n    ) -> Result<miroir_core::scatter::NodeResponse> {\n        // Check retry cache first if enabled\n        if let Some(ref cache) = self.retry_cache {\n            let cache_key = RetryCache::cache_key(body, node_id.as_str(), None);\n\n            if let Some(cached) = cache.get(&cache_key).await {\n                // Return cached response\n                return Ok(NodeResponse {\n                    node_id: node_id.clone(),\n                    body: cached.body,\n                    status: cached.status,\n                    headers: Vec::new(),\n                });\n            }\n        }\n\n        // No cache hit, send actual request\n        let timeout_dur = Duration::from_millis(self.node_timeout_ms);\n        let result = timeout(timeout_dur, async {\n            self.client\n                .send_to_node(topology, node_id, method, path, Some(body), headers)\n                .await\n        })\n        .await;\n\n        match result {\n            Ok(Ok(resp)) => {\n                let body_bytes = resp.body;\n                let status = resp.status;\n\n                // Cache successful responses\n                if let Some(ref cache) = self.retry_cache {\n                    // Cache only 2xx responses\n                    if (200..300).contains(&status) {\n                        let cache_key = RetryCache::cache_key(body, node_id.as_str(), None);\n                        let cached = CachedResponse {\n                            body: body_bytes.clone(),\n                            status,\n                            cached_at: std::time::Instant::now(),\n                        };\n                        cache.put(cache_key, cached).await;\n                    }\n                }\n\n                Ok(miroir_core::scatter::NodeResponse {\n                    node_id: node_id.clone(),\n                    body: body_bytes,\n                    status,\n                    headers: resp.headers,\n                })\n            }\n            _ => Err(MiroirError::Routing(format!(\n                \"request to node {} timed out\",\n                node_id.as_str()\n            ))),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl Scatter for HttpScatter {\n    async fn scatter(\n        &self,\n        topology: &Topology,\n        nodes: Vec<NodeId>,\n        request: ScatterRequest,\n        policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n    ) -> Result<ScatterResponse> {\n        // Fan out requests to all nodes in parallel\n        let futures: Vec<_> = nodes\n            .iter()\n            .map(|node_id| {\n                let node_id = node_id.clone();\n                let method = request.method.clone();\n                let path = request.path.clone();\n                let body = request.body.clone();\n                let headers = request.headers.clone();\n                let this = self.clone();\n                let topo = topology.clone();\n\n                async move {\n                    match this.send_to_node_with_cache(\n                        &topo,\n                        &node_id,\n                        &method,\n                        &path,\n                        &body,\n                        &headers,\n                    )\n                    .await\n                    {\n                        Ok(resp) => Ok(resp),\n                        Err(_) => Err(node_id),\n                    }\n                }\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        let results = futures::future::join_all(futures).await;\n\n        let mut successful = Vec::new();\n        let mut failed = Vec::new();\n\n        for result in results {\n            match result {\n                Ok(resp) => successful.push(resp),\n                Err(node_id) => failed.push(node_id),\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check if we should fail based on policy\n        if policy == UnavailableShardPolicy::Error && !failed.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MiroirError::Routing(format!(\n                \"{} nodes failed: {:?}\",\n                failed.len(),\n                failed\n            )));\n        }\n\n        Ok(ScatterResponse {\n            responses: successful,\n            failed,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use miroir_core::topology::{Node, Topology};\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_http_scatter_empty_nodes() {\n        let client = NodeClient::new(\"test-key\".to_string(), &Default::default());\n        let scatter = HttpScatter::new(client, 1000);\n        let topology = Topology::new(64, 1);\n\n        let request = ScatterRequest {\n            body: Vec::new(),\n            headers: Vec::new(),\n            method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n            path: \"/\".to_string(),\n        };\n\n        let result = scatter\n            .scatter(&topology, Vec::new(), request, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial)\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.responses.is_empty());\n        assert!(result.failed.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_http_scatter_timeout_handling() {\n        let client = NodeClient::new(\"test-key\".to_string(), &Default::default());\n        let scatter = HttpScatter::new(client, 1); // 1ms timeout\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 1);\n\n        // Add a node that will timeout\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"test-node\".to_string()),\n            \"http://invalid.example.com:7700\".to_string(),\n            0,\n        ));\n\n        let request = ScatterRequest {\n            body: Vec::new(),\n            headers: Vec::new(),\n            method: \"GET\".to_string(),\n            path: \"/\".to_string(),\n        };\n\n        let result = scatter\n            .scatter(\n                &topology,\n                vec![NodeId::new(\"test-node\".to_string())],\n                request,\n                UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n            )\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n\n        assert!(result.responses.is_empty());\n        assert_eq!(result.failed.len(), 1);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_http_scatter_error_policy() {\n        let client = NodeClient::new(\"test-key\".to_string(), &Default::default());\n        let scatter = HttpScatter::new(client, 1);\n        let mut topology = Topology::new(64, 1);\n\n        topology.add_node(Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(\"test-node\".to_string()),\n {\n52\t    #[allow(dead_code)]\n53\t    response: NodeResponse,\n54\t    cached_at: std::time::Instant,\n55\t}\n56\t\n57\timpl NodeClient {\n58\t    /// Create a new node client with connection pooling.\n59\t    pub fn new(node_master_key: String, server_config: &ServerConfig) -> Self {\n60\t        let client = Client::builder()\n61\t            .pool_max_idle_per_host(32)\n62\t            .pool_idle_timeout(Duration::from_secs(60))\n63\t            .timeout(Duration::from_millis(server_config.request_timeout_ms))\n64\t            .build()\n65\t            .expect(\"failed to create HTTP client\");\n66\t\n67\t        Self {\n68\t            client,\n69\t            node_master_key: Arc::new(node_master_key),\n70\t            retry_cache: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n71\t        }\n72\t    }\n73\t\n74\t    /// Send a request to a single node.\n75\t    pub async fn send_to_node(\n76\t        &self,\n77\t        topology: &Topology,\n78\t        node_id: &NodeId,\n79\t        method: &str,\n80\t        path: &str,\n81\t        body: Option<&[u8]>,\n82\t        headers: &[(String, String)],\n83\t    ) -> Result<NodeResponse> {\n84\t        let node = topology\n85\t            .node(node_id)\n86\t            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"node {} not found\", node_id.as_str())))?;\n87\t\n88\t        let url = format!(\"{}{}\", node.address, path);\n89\t\n90\t        let mut request = match method {\n91\t            \"GET\" => self.client.get(&url),\n92\t            \"POST\" => self.client.post(&url),\n93\t            \"PUT\" => self.client.put(&url),\n94\t            \"PATCH\" => self.client.patch(&url),\n95\t            \"DELETE\" => self.client.delete(&url),\n96\t            _ => {\n97\t                return Err(MiroirError::Routing(format!(\n98\t                    \"unsupported HTTP method: {method}\",\n99\t                )))\n100\t            }\n101\t        };\n102\t\n103\t        // Add node master key header\n104\t        request = request.header(\n105\t            \"Authorization\",\n106\t            format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.node_master_key.as_str()),\n107\t        );\n108\t\n109\t        // Add custom headers\n110\t        for (key, value) in headers {\n111\t            request = \"application/json\");\n117\t            request = request.body(body_bytes.to_vec());\n118\t        }\n119\t\n120\t        let response = request.send().await.map_err(|e| {\n121\t            MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"request to node {} failed: {e}\", node_id.as_str(),))\n122\t        })?;\n123\t\n124\t        let status = response.status().as_u16();\n125\t\n126\t        // Collect HTTP client for forwarding requests to Meilisearch nodes.\n//!\n//! Implements connection pooling, retries, and orchestrator-side\n//! retry cache for idempotency (plan §4 note on scatter.retry_on_timeout).\n\nuse miroir_core::config::ServerConfig;\nuse miroir_core::topology::{NodeId, Topology};\nuse miroir_core::{MiroirError, Result};\nuse reqwest::Client;\nuse serde_json::Value;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::Duration;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Node response with status code and body (as Vec<u8> for scatter compatibility).\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct NodeResponse {\n    pub node_id: NodeId,\n    pub status: u16,\n    pub body: Vec<u8>,\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    pub headers: Vec<(String, String)>,\n}\n\nimpl NodeResponse {\n    /// Convert body to JSON value.\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    pub fn body_json(&self) -> Result<Value> {\n        serde_json::from_slice(&self.body).map_err(|e| MiroirError::Json(e))\n    }\n}\n\n/// HTTP client for scatter-gather requests to Meilisearch nodes.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct NodeClient {\n    /// Reqwest client with connection pooling.\n    client: Client,\n\n    /// Node master key for authentication.\n    node_master_key: Arc<String>,\n\n    /// Orchestrator-side retry cache for idempotency.\n    /// Key: sha256(request_body || target_node || idempotency_key)\n    /// Value: cached terminal response\n    retry_cache: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, CachedResponse>>>,\n}\n\n/// Cached response for retry deduplication.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\nstruct CachedResponse {\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    response: NodeResponse,\n    cached_at: std::time::Instant,\n}\n\nimpl NodeClient {\n    /// Create a new node client with connection pooling.\n    pub fn new(node_master_key: String, server_config: &ServerConfig) -> Self {\n        let client = Client::builder()\n            .pool_max_idle_per_host(32)\n            .pool_idle_timeout(Duration::from_secs(60))\n            .timeout(Duration::from_millis(server_config.request_timeout_ms))\n            .build()\n            .expect(\"failed to create HTTP client\");\n\n        Self {\n            client,\n            node_master_key: Arc::new(node_master_key),\n            retry_cache: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Send a request to a single node.\n    pub async fn send_to_node(\n        &self,\n        topology: &Topology,\n        node_id: &NodeId,\n        method: &str,\n        path: &str,\n        body: Option<&[u8]>,\n        headers: &[(String, String)],\n    ) -> Result<NodeResponse> {\n        let node = topology\n            .node(node_id)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"node {} not found\", node_id.as_str())))?;\n\n        let url = format!(\"{}{}\", node.address, path);\n\n        let mut request = match method {\n            \"GET\" => self.client.get(&url),\n            \"POST\" => self.client.post(&url),\n            \"PUT\" => self.client.put(&url),\n            \"PATCH\" => self.client.patch(&url),\n            \"DELETE\" => self.client.delete(&url),\n            _ => {\n                return Err(MiroirError::Routing(format!(\n                    \"unsupported HTTP method: {method}\",\n                )))\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Add node master key header\n        request = request.header(\n            \"Authorization\",\n            format!(\"Bearer {}\", self.node_master_key.as_str()),\n        );\n\n        // Add custom headers\n        for (key, value) in headers {\n            request = request.header(key, value);\n        }\n\n        // Add body if present\n        if let Some(body_bytes) = body {\n            request = request.header(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\");\n            request = request.body(body_bytes.to_vec());\n        }\n\n        let response = request.send().await.map_err(|e| {\n            MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"request to node {} failed: {e}\", node_id.as_str(),))\n        })?;\n\n        let status = response.status().as_u16();\n\n        // Collect response headers\n        let response_headers: Vec<(String, String)> = response\n            .headers()\n            .iter()\n            .filter_map(|(name, value)| {\n                value\n                    .to_str()\n                    .ok()\n                    .map(|v| (name.as_str().to_string(), v.to_string()))\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        let body_bytes = response\n            .bytes()\n            .await\n            .map_err(|e| MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"failed to read response body: {e}\")))?;\n\n        Ok(NodeResponse {\n            node_id: node_id.clone(),\n            status,\n            body: body_bytes.to_vec(),\n            headers: response_headers,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Send requests to multiple nodes in parallel.\n    pub async fn send_to_many(\n        &self,\n        topology: &Topology,\n        node_ids: &[NodeId],\n        method: &str,\n        path: &str,\n        body: Option<&[u8]>,\n        headers: &[(String, String)],\n    ) -> Vec<Result<NodeResponse>> {\n        let futures: Vec<_> = node_ids\n            .iter()\n            .map(|node_id| self.send_to_node(topology, node_id, method, path, body, headers))\n            .collect();\n\n        futures::future::join_all(futures).await\n    }\n\n    /// Prune old entries from the retry cache.\n    pub async fn prune_retry_cache(&self, max_age: Duration) {\n        let mut cache = self.retry_cache.write().await;\n        let now = std::time::Instant::now();\n        cache.retain(|_, entry| now.duration_since(entry.cached_at) < max_age);\n Orchestrator-side retry cache for idempotency (plan §4).\n//!\n//! Key: sha256(batch || target_node || idempotency_key_or_mtask)\n//!\n//! This cache prevents duplicate writes when retrying timed-out requests\n//! to nodes. It stores terminal responses (success or definitive failure)\n//! for a configurable TTL.\n\nuse sha2::{Digest, Sha256};\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, Instant};\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n\n/// Cached response from a node.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct CachedResponse {\n    /// Response body.\n    pub body: Vec<u8>,\n    /// HTTP status code.\n    pub status: u16,\n    /// When this cache entry was created.\n    pub cached_at: Instant,\n}\n\nimpl CachedResponse {\n    /// Check if this entry is still valid (not expired).\n    pub fn is_valid(&self, ttl: Duration) -> bool {\n        self.cached_at.elapsed() < ttl\n    }\n}\n\n/// Retry cache for idempotency.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct RetryCache {\n    entries: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, CachedResponse>>>,\n    default_ttl: Duration,\n}\n\nimpl RetryCache {\n    /// Create a new retry cache with the specified default TTL.\n    pub fn new(default_ttl: Duration) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            entries: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n            default_ttl,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Generate cache key from request components.\n    ///\n    /// Key format: sha256(batch || target_node || idempotency_key_or_mtask)\n    pub fn cache_key(\n        batch: &[u8],\n        target_node: &str,\n        idempotency_key: Option<&str>,\n    ) -> String {\n        let mut hasher = Sha256::new();\n\n        // Include batch\n        hasher.update(batch);\n\n        // Include target node\n        hasher.update(target_node.as_bytes());\n\n        // Include idempotency key or use empty string\n        if let Some(key) = idempotency_key {\n            hasher.update(key.as_bytes());\n        }\n\n        let result = hasher.finalize();\n        hex::encode(result)\n    }\n\n    /// Get a cached response if it exists and is still valid.\n    pub async fn get(&self, key: &str) -> Option<CachedResponse> {\n        let cache = self.entries.read().await;\n        cache.get(key).and_then(|entry| {\n            if entry.is_valid(self.default_ttl) {\n                Some(entry.clone())\n            } else {\n                None\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Store a response in the cache.\n    pub async fn put(&self, key: String, response: CachedResponse) {\n        let mut cache = self.entries.write().await;\n        cache.insert(key, response);\n    }\n\n    /// Remove expired entries from the cache.\n    pub async fn prune(&self) {\n        let mut cache = self.entries.write().await;\n        let now = Instant::now();\n\n        cache.retain(|_, entry| {\n            now.duration_since(entry.cached_at) < self.default_ttl\n        });\n    }\n\n    /// Clear all entries from the cache.\n    pub async fn clear(&self) {\n        let mut cache = self.entries.write().await;\n        cache.clear();\n    }\n\n    /// Get the number of entries in the cache.\n    pub async fn len(&self) -> usize {\n        let cache = self.entries.read().await;\n        cache.len()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the cache is empty.\n    pub async fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {\n        let cache = self.entries.read().await;\n        cache.is_empty()\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_cache_key_generation() {\n        let batch = b\"test_batch\";\n        let target_node = \"node1\";\n        let idempotency_key = Some(\"key123\");\n\n        let key1 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, target_node, idempotency_key);\n        let key2 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, target_node, idempotency_key);\n\n        // Same inputs should produce same key\n        assert_eq!(key1, key2);\n\n        // Different inputs should produce different keys\n        let key3 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, \"node2\", idempotency_key);\n        assert_ne!(key1, key3);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_cache_key_without_idempotency() {\n        let batch = b\"test_batch\";\n        let target_node = \"node1\";\n\n        let key1 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, target_node, None);\n        let key2 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, target_node, None);\n\n        // Same inputs should produce same key\n        assert_eq!(key1, key2);\n\n        // With and without idempotency key should be different\n        let key3 = RetryCache::cache_key(batch, target_node, Some(\"key123\"));\n        assert_ne!(key1, key3);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_cache_put_get() {\n        let cache = RetryCache::new(Duration::from_secs(60));\n\n        let response = CachedResponse {\n            body: b\"test_response\".to_vec(),\n            status: 200,\n            cached_at: Instant::now(),\n        };\n\n        let key = \"test_key\".to_string();\n        cache.put(key.clone(), response.clone()).await;\n\n        let retrieved = cache.get(&key).await;\n        assert!(retrieved.is_some());\n        let retrieved = retrieved.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(retrieved.body, b\"test_response\");\n        assert_eq!(retrieved.status, 200);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_cache_expiration() {\n        let cache = RetryCache::new(Duration::from_millis(100));\n\n        let response = CachedResponse {\n            body: b\"test_response\".to_vec(),\n            status: 200,\n            cached_at: Instant::now(),\n        };\n\n        let key = \"test_key\".to_string();\n        cache.put(key.clone(), response).await;\n\n        // Should be valid immediately\n        let retrieved = cache.get(&key).await;\n        assert!(retrieved.is_some());\n\n        // Wait for expiration\n        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(150)).await;\n\n        // Should be expired now\n        let retrieved = cache.get(&key).await;\n        assert!(retrieved.is_none());\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_cache_prune() {\n        let cache = RetryCache::new(Duration::from_millis(100));\n\n        let response1 = CachedResponse {\n            body: b\"test_response1\".to_vec(),\n            status: 200,\n            cached_at: Instant::now(),\n        };\n\n        let response2 = CachedResponse {\n            body: b\"test_response2\".to_vec(),\n            status: 200,\n            cached_at: Instant::now(),\n        };\n\n        cache.put(\"key1\".to_string(), response1).await;\n   cache.prune().await;\n\n        // Should be empty after pruning\n        assert_eq!(cache.len().await, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_cache_clear() {\n        let cache = RetryCache::new(Duration::from_secs(60));\n\n        let response = CachedResponse {\n            body: b\"test_response\".to_vec(),\n            status: 200,\n            cached_at: Instant::now(),\n cache.clear().await;\n\n        assert_eq!(cache.len().await, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_cached_response_is_valid() {\n        let response = CachedResponse {\n            body: vec![],\n {\n        Self { state }\n    }\n\n    /// Create an index on all nodes.\n    pub async fn create_index(&self, uid: &str, primary_key: Option<&str>) -> Result<IndexResult> {\n        let topology = self.state.topology().await;\n\n        // Prepare request body\n        let mut body = json!({\n            \"uid\": uid,\n        });\n\n        if let Some(pk) = primary_key {\n            body[\"primaryKey\"] = json!(pk);\n        }\n\n        let body_bytes = serde_json::to_vec(&body).unwrap();\n\n        // Broadcast to all nodes\n        let mut node_tasks = HashMap::new();\n        let mut failed_nodes = Vec::new();\n\n        for node in topology.nodes() {\n            match self\n                .state\n                .client\n                .send_to_node(\n                    &topology,\n                    &node.id,\n                    \"POST\",\n                    \"/indexes\",\n                    Some(&body_bytes),\n                    &[],\n                )\n                .await\n            {\n                Ok(resp) if (200..300).contains(&resp.status) => {\n                    if let Some(task_uid) = resp.body.get(\"taskUid\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()) {\n                        node_tasks.insert(node.id.as_str().to_string(), task_uid);\n                    }\n                }\n                _ => {\n                    failed_nodes.push(node.id.as_str().to_string());\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        if !failed_nodes.is_empty() {\n            // Rollback: delete from successful nodes\n            for node_id in node_tasks.keys() {\n                let _ = self\n                    .state\n                    .client\n                    .send_to_node(\n                        &topology,\n                        &node_id.clone().into(),\n                        \"DELETE\",\n                        &format!(\"/indexes/{}\", uid),\n                        None,\n                        &[],\n                    )\n                    .await;\n            }\n\n            return Err(MiroirError::Routing(format!(\n                \"Failed to create index on nodes: {:?}\",\n                failed_nodes\n            )));\n        }\n\n        // Add _miroir_shard to filterable attributes\n        self.add_miroir_shard_filterable(uid).await?;\n\n        let miroir_task_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", Uuid::new_v4());\n\n        Ok(IndexResult {\n            miroir_task_id,\n            node_tasks,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Delete an index from all nodes.\n    pub async fn failed_nodes.push(node.id.as_str().to_string());\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        if !failed_nodes.is_empty() {\n            return Err(MiroirError::Routing(format!(\n                \"Failed to delete index on nodes: {:?}\",\n                failed_nodes\n            )));\n        }\n\n        let miroir_task_id = format!(\"mtask-{}\", Uuid::new_v4());\n\n        Ok(IndexResult {\n            miroir_task_id,\n            node_tasks,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Get aggregated stats for an index.\n    pub async fn get_stats(&self, uid: &str) -> Result<Value> {\n        let topology = self.state.topology().await;\n\n        let mut total_documents = 0u64;\n        let mut field_distribution: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n        let mut failed_nodes = Vec::new();\n\n        for node in topology.nodes() {\n            match self\n                .state\n                .client\n                .send_to_node(\n                    &topology,\n                    &node.id,\n                    \"GET\",\n                    &format!(\"/indexes/{}/stats\", uid),\n                    None,\n                    &[],\n                )\n                .await\n            {\n                Ok(resp) if (200..300).contains(&resp.status) => {\n                    // Sum numberOfDocuments\n                    if let Some(count) = resp.body.get(\"numberOfDocuments\").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()) {\n                        total_documents += count;\n                    }\n\n                    // Merge fieldDistribution\n                    if let Some(fields) = resp.body.get(\"fieldDistribution\").and_then(|v| v.as_object()) {\n                        for (field, count) in fields {\n                            let count_val = count.as_u64().unwrap_or(0);\n                            *field_distribution.entry(field.clone()).or_insert(0) += count_val;\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                _ => {\n                    failed_nodes.push(node.id.as_str().to_string());\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        if failed_nodes.len() > topology.nodes().count() / 2 {\n            return Err(MiroirError::Routing(format!(\n                \"Failed to get stats from majority of nodes: {:?}\",\n                failed_nodes\n            )));\n        }\n\n        Ok(json!({\n            \"numberOfDocuments\": total_documents,\n            \"fieldDistribution\": field_distribution,\n        }))\n    }\n\n    /// Add _miroir_shard to filterable attributes.\n    async fn add_miroir_shard_filterable(&self, uid: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        let topology = self.state.topology().await;\n\n        // Get current settings\n        let first_node = topology.nodes().next();\n        if let Some(node) = first_node {\n            if let Ok(resp) = self\n                .state\n                .client\n                .send_to_node(\n                    &topology,\n                    &node.id,\n                    \"GET\",\n                    &format!(\"/indexes/{}/settings/filterable-attributes\", uid),\n                    None,\n                    &[],\n                )\n                .await\n            {\n                if let Some(attrs) = resp.body.as_array() {\n                    let mut attrs_vec: Vec<String> = attrs\n                        .iter()\n                        .filter_map(|v| v.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string()))\n                        .collect();\n\n                    if !attrs_vec.contains(&\"_miroir_shard\".to_string()) {\n                        attrs_vec.push(\"_miroir_shard\".to_string());\n\n                        let body = serde_json::to_vec(&attrs_vec).unwrap();\n\n                        // Broadcast to all nodes\n                        for node in topology.nodes() {\n                            let _ = self\n                                .state\n                                .client\n                                .send_to_node(\n                                    &topology,\n                                    &node.id,\n                                    \"PUT\",\n                                    &format!(\"/indexes/{}/settings/filterable-attributes\", uid),\n                                    Some(&body),\n                                    &[],\n                                )\n                                .await;\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result of an index operation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct IndexResult {\n    pub miroir_task_id: String,\n    pub node_tasks: HashMap<String, u64>,\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\n\n    #[tokio::test]\n    async fn test_index_result_creation() {\n        let usize,\n        limit: usize,\n    ) -> Result<SearchResult> {\n        let topology = self.state.topology().await;\n        let shard_count = self.state.config.shards;\n        let rf = self.state.config.replication_factor as usize;\n        let replica_groups = topology.replica_group_count();\n\n        // Select query group\n        let query_seq = self.state.next_query_seq();\n        let group_id = router::query_group(query_seq, replica_groups);\n\n        let group = topology\n            .group(group_id)\n            .ok_or_else(|| MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"Group {} not found\", group_id)))?;\n\n        // Build covering set\n        let covering = router::covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, query_seq);\n\n        // Deduplicate nodes\n        let unique_nodes: std::collections::HashSet<_> = covering.into_iter().collect();\n\n        // Prepare search query\n        let mut query_with_score = query.clone();\n        if let Some(obj) = query_with_score.as_object_mut() {\n            obj.insert(\"showRankingScore\".to_string(), json!(true));\n        }\n\n        let body = serde_json::to_vec(&query_with_score).unwrap();\n        let path = format!(\"/indexes/{}/search\", index);\n\n        let request = ScatterRequest {\n            body,\n            headers: vec![],\n            method: \"POST\".to_string(),\n            path,\n        };\n\n        // Get policy from config\n        let policy = match self.state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n            \"error\" => responses\n        let mut shard_responses: Vec<ShardResponse> = Vec::new();\n        let mut degraded_shards = Vec::new();\n\n        for node_resp in response.responses {\n            // Parse response as shard response (all shards from this node)\n            shard_responses.push(ShardResponse {\n                shard_id: 0, // We'll merge all responses together\n                body: serde_json::from_slice(&node_resp.body).unwrap_or(json!({})),\n                success: true,\n            });\n        }\n\n        for failed_node in &response.failed {\n            degraded_shards.push(failed_node.as_str().to_string());\n        }\n\n        // Check if client requested ranking score\n        let client_requested_score = query\n            .get(\"showRankingScore\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_bool())\n            .unwrap_or(false);\n\n        // Merge results\n        let merged = self\n            .merger\n            .merge(shard_responses, offset, limit, client_requested_score)?;\n\n        // Build response\n        let mut result = json!({\n            \"hits\": merged.hits,\n            \"processingTimeMs\": merged.processing_time_ms,\n            \"query\": query,\n        });\n\n        if !merged.facets.is_null() {\n            if let Some(obj) = result.as_object_mut() {\n                obj.insert(\"facetDistribution\".to_string(), merged.facets);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Add estimatedTotalHits if present\n        if merged.total_hits > 0 {\n            if let Some(obj) = result.as_object_mut() {\n                obj.insert(\"estimatedTotalHits\".to_string(), json!(merged.total_hits));\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(SearchResult {\n            body: result,\n            degraded: merged.degraded,\n            degraded_shards,\n        })\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result of a search operation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct SearchResult {\n failed_nodes.push(node_id);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check quorum - write succeeds if at least one group met quorum\n        let degraded_groups = self.check_quorum(&group_quorum, if let Some(pk) = resp.body.get(\"primaryKey\").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n                return Ok(pk.to_string());\n            }\n        }\n\n        Err(MiroirError::Routing(format!(\n MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"Primary key '{}' not found in document\", pk))\n        })?;\n\n        Ok(value.to_string())\n    }\n\n    fn check_quorum(&self, group_quorum: &HashMap<u32, GroupQuorum>, topology: &Topology) -> Vec<u32> {\n        let mut degraded = Vec::new();\n\n        for (group_id, quorum) in group_quorum {\n            if !quorum.met_quorum() {\n                degraded.push(*group_id);\n            }\n        }\n\n        degraded\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result of a document write operation.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct WriteResult {\n    pub
